fix: use guild.channels.fetch() for private channel lookup

guild.channels.cache only contains previously-fetched channels.
Private channels like 'coaches' weren't cached, so lookups failed.
Now uses guild.channels.fetch() which queries the Discord API for
all channels the bot has access to (including private ones).

Applied to: all-team override, channel overrides, handleAddEntry,
handleSetAllTeamChannel, and ChannelMapper.findChannel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add lab docs and fix vault path (~/.bloom/ → ~/bloom/)

Add docs/lab/ with four exploratory design documents:
- EMERGENCE.md: local semantic embeddings for emergence detection
- LIVE_VIEWS.md: composable query language (BQL) for live views
- JOURNAL_REDESIGN.md: single journal.md with auto-rotation
- TIME_TRAVEL.md: git-backed history via gix with cached day views

Fix all ~/.bloom/ references to ~/bloom/ across main design docs
(GOALS, ARCHITECTURE, DESIGN_DECISIONS, USE_CASES, DEBUGGABILITY).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: Delete Account — allow users to permanently delete their account with two-step confirmation (#48)

* feat: [US-001] - Model account deletion in TLA+ spec with new invariants

Add DeleteProfile(uid) action that removes uid from createdProfiles,
frees the username reservation, and resets hasPicture. Add two new
invariants (NoOrphanedAuthUsers, DeletionIsComplete) ensuring no
orphaned state after deletion. TLC verifies 184,321 states with
zero violations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-002] - Add DELETE /users/me API endpoint with full data cleanup

Implements account deletion endpoint that:
- Blocks deletion if user is admin of BurnSquads (409)
- Deletes user profile, username reservation, workouts, friends,
  friend requests, burn buddies, burn buddy requests, squad join requests
- Removes user from squad memberUids arrays
- Handles group workouts (delete if <2 members remain)
- Deletes profile picture from Storage (ignores 404)
- Deletes Firebase Auth user record as final step
- Each cleanup step is wrapped in try/catch for resilience
- Workouts batch-deleted in chunks of 500

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-003] - Add unit tests for DELETE /users/me endpoint

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: [US-004] - Add delete account UI to web account page with two-step confirmation

- Add Delete Account section with red/destructive styling at bottom of account page
- Two-step confirmation: modal dialog with warning text, then re-authentication
- Email/password users: prompted for password via reauthenticateWithCredential
- Google/social users: re-authenticated via reauthenticateWithPopup
- Calls apiDelete('/users/me') after successful re-auth
- Handles 409 (squad admin) with error message listing squads
- On success: signs out, clears session, redirects to /login
- Loading spinner shown during deletion
- ApiError class added to api.ts for structured error handling with status/body

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

containing the correct "agigante80/AgentGate" repository.

Adding explicit owner/repo to copilot-instructions.md grounds the model.

* feat: [US-002] - Update CrossDomainInvariants.tla for cascade deletion and CDI-8

- Added CDI-8: NoBuddyWithoutFriendship invariant ensuring no burn buddy exists without active friendship
- Updated module header comments to include CDI-8 and reflect cascade behavior

  and delete the user's override message for cleanliness

feat: masternode tier detection, daemon conf guide, and collateral locking

Problems fixed:
1. masternode.conf was never written for entries that existed before the
   sync fix — now sync_masternode_conf() is called at wallet init when
   entries are loaded from the DB.
2. Collateral UTXOs are now locked at wallet init for all existing entries,
   preventing them from being accidentally spent.
3. The UI had no tier indicator — users couldn't tell if their collateral
   qualified for Bronze/Silver/Gold.
4. No guidance on how to configure the masternode daemon server.

Changes:
- wallet_db.rs: add masternode_tier_from_satoshis() (Bronze/Silver/Gold
  thresholds: 1K/10K/100K TIME) and to_daemon_conf_line() (4-field format
  for the timed daemon)
- service.rs: call sync_masternode_conf + lock_collateral for all entries
  when loaded at wallet init
- masternodes.rs: complete UI overhaul
  - Tier Requirements & Setup Guide collapsible header with thresholds and
    step-by-step instructions
  - Live tier preview in the add form (looks up UTXO amount in state.utxos)
  - Right-click Paste context menu on TXID and payout address fields
  - Tier badge (🥇/🥈/🟫) displayed next to each entry alias
  - Collateral amount shown per entry
  - 'Daemon Conf' button copies the daemon-format line to clipboard with
    tooltip showing the exact text and where to paste it on the server
  - Renamed 'Register On-Chain' → '▶ Start On-Chain' with descriptive tooltip

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add agent parameter to session creation for pre-selecting custom agents (#722)

Add an optional `agent` field to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig across
all four SDKs (Node.js, Python, Go, .NET) that allows specifying which custom
agent should be active when the session starts.

Previously, users had to create a session and then make a separate
`session.rpc.agent.select()` call to activate a specific custom agent. This
change allows setting the agent directly in the session config, equivalent to
passing `--agent <name>` in the Copilot CLI.

The `agent` value must match the `name` of one of the agents defined in
`customAgents`.

Changes:
- Node.js: Added `agent?: string` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.ts for both session.create and session.resume RPC calls
- Python: Added `agent: str` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.py for both create and resume payloads
- Go: Added `Agent string` to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  wired in client.go for both request types
- .NET: Added `Agent` property to SessionConfig and ResumeSessionConfig,
  updated copy constructors, CreateSessionRequest/ResumeSessionRequest records,
  and CreateSessionAsync/ResumeSessionAsync call sites
- Docs: Added "Selecting an Agent at Session Creation" section with examples
  in all 4 languages to custom-agents.md, updated session-persistence.md and
  getting-started.md
- Tests: Added unit tests verifying agent parameter is forwarded in both
  session.create and session.resume RPC calls

Closes #317, closes #410, closes #547

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add .txt file indexing as plaintext file-only nodes

Map .txt extension to 'plaintext' in EXTENSION_TO_LANGUAGE. Since no
tree-sitter grammar exists for plaintext, files gracefully fall through
to _create_file_only_node() which creates a file-level node with full
content — enabling smart content summaries and embedding search.

Also includes ruff formatting fixes from just fft.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: faster all-team batch, no arg truncation, multi-format 2NR parsing

- Batch flush: 2min→30s after last email, 10min→3min hard cap
- All-team report: removed 60-char argument truncation
- Argument regex: supports dash (2NR- Set Col K), colon (2NR: T),
  period, and space separators for both 1AC and 2NR patterns
- Updated all internal regex references (_findNegDocRound, doc link)
- 3 new tests for dash/colon/period argument formats

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: reorganize docs/ into intent-based sections (#723)

* docs: reorganize docs/ around user intent

Restructure the docs folder from an organic flat layout into
intent-based sections that are easier to navigate:

- setup/     — get it running (was guides/setup/, moved up one level)
- auth/      — configure access (unchanged)
- features/  — build things (was guides/, scoped to SDK capabilities)
- hooks/     — API reference per hook (unchanged, overview.md → index.md)
- troubleshooting/ — fix problems (new, consolidates debugging + compatibility)
- observability/   — monitoring (new, houses OpenTelemetry docs)

Key changes:
- Add docs/index.md as top-level table of contents with persona routing
- Add docs/features/index.md as feature guide overview
- Remove duplicate guides/setup/byok.md (auth/byok.md is source of truth)
- Rename hooks/overview.md → hooks/index.md for consistency
- Move mcp/overview.md → features/mcp.md, mcp/debugging.md → troubleshooting/
- Update all internal cross-references (verified zero broken links)
- Expand README Quick Links to surface more documentation sections

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: move Microsoft Agent Framework to new integrations/ section

Create a dedicated integrations/ section at the bottom of the docs
hierarchy for platform/framework-specific guides. Move MAF out of
features/ since it's an integration pattern, not an SDK capability.

This makes it easy to add future guides for other platforms alongside MAF.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(openClaw): host hardening for OpenClaw (#39)

* feat(openClaw): host hardening for OpenClaw provisioning

- Added scripts/setup-worker-node.sh for host-level hardening (KSM, swap, THP).
- Implemented egress allowlist and network rate limiting in network.py.
- Updated VM facade and core types to support OpenClaw provisioning flows.
- Enhanced doctor.py with environment validation for worker nodes.
- Verified changes with unit tests for VM and type configurations.

* fix: allow underscores in vm_id and update drive names

* fix(tests): mock worker-node security checks to prevent CI failures in doctor tests (#51)

* Initial plan

* fix(tests): mock worker-node security checks in doctor tests to prevent CI failures

The _check_swap_disabled, _check_thp_disabled and related functions read real
system files. On CI swap is active and THP is 'always', causing test failures.
Patch those functions with passing stubs in the two affected firecracker tests.

Co-authored-by: aniketmaurya <21018714+aniketmaurya@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: aniketmaurya <21018714+aniketmaurya@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix OpenClaw review findings for network, doctor, and worker setup

* Make egress allowlist updates atomic and fail-closed

* Fix review findings for OpenClaw runtime PR (#52)

* Fix OpenClaw review findings for network, doctor, and worker setup

* Make egress allowlist updates atomic and fail-closed

* fix(build,network): openclaw detach fix, loopfs timeout, docker coexistence

build.py — systemctl proxy script:
- Set HOME=/home/node to prevent EACCES chdir when SSH inherits HOME=/root
- Add </dev/null so openclaw does not inherit the SSH channel stdin
- Add & disown so non-interactive bash (su -c) exits immediately without
  waiting for the backgrounded openclaw process — fixes systemctl start
  openclaw SSH timeout in the reconciler

build.py — loopfs extract:
- Scale extract timeout: max(300, rootfs_size_mb // 8) seconds
  Tar-extracting node_modules onto a loop ext4 is inode-bound and can
  easily exceed 30 s on pd-standard disks for 4 GB+ images

setup-worker-node.sh — section 6:
- Add Docker coexistence check/apply for DOCKER-USER iptables rules
  so SmolVM tap+ traffic is not blocked by Docker's FORWARD policy

* fix(build): scale umount timeout same as extract

After extracting 4 GB+ of node_modules onto a loop-mounted ext4,
the kernel must flush a large dirty-page cache to disk before umount
can complete. On pd-standard this exceeds the 30 s default timeout.
Pass the same extract_timeout (max(300, rootfs_size_mb // 8)) to the
umount call so both operations use a size-scaled limit.

* chore: address PR #39 review findings

- Use awk to safely strip /etc/fstab swap entries
- Provide vendor-accurate KVM nx_huge_pages modprobe hints
- Enforce strict fail on missing DOCKER-USER chain
- Extract shared initialization logic in build.py to _base_init_script using proper f-string escaping
- Sanitize extra_packages via regex to prevent Docker RUN injection
- Pass ssh_password securely to Docker via --build-arg
- Implement deterministic sha256 cache fingerprinting for image inputs

* fix(provisioning): replace non-portable grep -oP with sed

The grep -oP flag relies on PCRE which is unsupported on macOS and minimal
Linux distros. Replaced reading THP values with a portable sed extraction
to fix cross-platform parsing errors.

* fix(worker): harden THP parsing and atomically update fstab (#54)

* Initial plan

* fix(worker): harden THP parsing and fstab updates

Co-authored-by: aniketmaurya <21018714+aniketmaurya@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: aniketmaurya <21018714+aniketmaurya@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(build): add fingerprint caching support to image builder

* chore: remove setup-worker-node.sh from this branch

* feat(cli): enhance doctor output with colors and concise fix suggestions

This adds ANSI color codes to the doctor report for better readability, extracts remediation instructions into a separate 'Fix:' line, and updates the regex assertions in test_doctor.py to account for the new format.

showing which of the last 7 days had group workouts, including date,
hasWorkout flag, groupWorkoutId, and dayLabel. calculateGroupStats()
also populates last7Days. Added 7 new unit tests covering all scenarios.

updated in US-002. Updated dashboard EnrichedBurnBuddy/EnrichedBurnSquad

endpoint to track and return highestActiveStreakLast7Days.

activity. Supports orange/violet color variants, clickable segments
linking to group workouts, day labels, and streak count in center.

shows description text on hover (desktop) and focus/tap (mobile).

commit.message:
feat(hot-reload): make Harmony/MonoMod opt-in via SAGEFS_HOT_RELOAD=1

Remove unconditional --multiemit- from FSI sessions. The flag disabled
type redefinition in the REPL for all users, even those not using
browser hot-reload — an unacceptable regression.

Changes:
- WorkerConfig gains HotReloadEnabled: bool (env: SAGEFS_HOT_RELOAD=1)
- buildWorkerSpawnConfig gains hotReload: bool (6th param)
- solutionToFsiArgs gains hotReload: bool; --multiemit- is conditional
- DirectoryConfig eval session: remove --multiemit- (no Harmony there)
- ActorArgs gains HotReloadEnabled: bool (default false)
- mkAppStateActor and createFsiSession thread hotReload through
- Fix hardcoded HotReloadEnabled = true in StartupConfig init
- WorkerMain.fs: gate DevReloadInjector.install() behind HotReloadEnabled
- SessionManager.fs: pass false as hotReload to buildWorkerSpawnConfig

Default behaviour: no --multiemit-, no Harmony, type redefinition works.
Opt-in: SAGEFS_HOT_RELOAD=1 enables DevReload browser auto-refresh and
method detouring, with the documented tradeoff that type redefinition
is disabled in that session.

Tests: update 6 buildWorkerSpawnConfig call sites, 2 WorkerConfig record
literals, 3 solutionToFsiArgs call sites; add hotReloadWorkerConfigTests
and hotReloadSpawnConfigTests; add 2 new solutionToFsiArgs cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

validation: R14 flywheel — 21 bugs found, 9 doc fixes applied

Round 14 full validation: 5 questions targeting under-tested modules.

commit.message:
docs: frame wrap.language as extensible language dispatch key

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: bypass LangChain for Gemini tool calling — preserve thought_signature

Root cause: Gemini 3 Flash requires thought_signature in function call
round-trips. LangChain langchain-google-genai strips this field → 400 error.

Fix: Add _call_with_tools_gemini() using google-genai SDK directly
(same pattern as existing _call_with_tools_doubao). This properly:
- Converts OpenAI-format tools → Gemini FunctionDeclaration
- Converts OpenAI-format messages → Gemini Content with thought_signature
- Captures thought_signature from model response
- Preserves it in assistant messages for next turn
- Uses thinking_level=MINIMAL for cost efficiency

Also: canvas_chat_service now includes function name in tool result
messages for reliable Gemini FunctionResponse construction.

Model restored to gemini-3-flash-preview.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: skip anonymous TypeScript nodes (SKIP_WHEN_ANONYMOUS)

Add SKIP_WHEN_ANONYMOUS frozenset to ast_parser_impl.py that prevents
node creation for 10 tree-sitter kinds when unnamed, while still
recursing into children to extract named nested structures.

Eliminates 13,649 anonymous @line.column nodes (58.6% of graph) from
TypeScript/TSX codebases. Chainglass benchmark: 23,283 → 9,636 nodes,
451 MB → 175 MB graph, SNR 31.8% → 100%.

Includes:
- SKIP_WHEN_ANONYMOUS constant (10 ts_kinds)
- Conditional skip-and-recurse block in _extract_nodes()
- 16 TDD tests (test_ast_parser_skip_anonymous.py)
- TypeScript fixtures (anonymous_callbacks.ts, anonymous_bodies.ts)
- Full plan docs, impact analysis, benchmarks

1561 tests pass, 0 regressions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(rust): add wasm support (#1062)

Very much unoptimised, maybe we are even not going to merge this.

Not sure

Current fairly unoptimised version is:
```
156K	pkg/mlt.wasm
52K	pkg/mlt.wasm.gz
```

This compares to the JS version:

```
64K	bundle.js
20K	bundle.js.gz
```

This is a bit of a flase comparison, wasm can be streamed in as fast as
the network can hold, while JS needs to be parsed & such.

---------

Signed-off-by: dependabot[bot] <support@github.com>

dashboard: Enhance network tab with type, height, scroll, and in/out counts

- Add tier, height, active fields to PeerInfo struct
- Show out/in connection breakdown in network info box
- New columns: status dot (●/○), Type (masternode tier), Height (block height)
- Color rows: green=active outbound, cyan=active inbound, gray=inactive
- Sort: active masternodes first, then non-masternodes, then by ping
- Scrollable peer table with ↑↓ keys (no more 20-peer hard cap)
- Remove take(20) limit — all peers visible with scroll

commit.message:
fix: revert gemini model to 2.5-flash (Gemini 3 thought_signature incompatible with LangChain)

Gemini 3 Flash requires thought_signature in function call responses,
but langchain-google-genai 4.2.x doesn't preserve it → 400 errors.
Revert to gemini-2.5-flash until LangChain adds support.
See: https://github.com/langchain-ai/langchain-google/issues/1364

commit.message:
Add `member organizations` command with `orgs` alias

Implement the `trololo member organizations` subcommand (alias `orgs`) that
lists the Trello workspaces (organizations) a member belongs to via
GET /members/{id}/organizations.

- Add Organization model (id, name, displayName, url, idBoards)
- Add getMemberOrganizations(memberId:) endpoint to MembersAPI
- Add Organizations subcommand to MemberCommand with table output
- Add Organization decoding tests (full, minimal, round-trip)
- Add endpoint path tests for getMemberOrganizations
